articles.title: Closing the gap between animal movement and robotic control
articles.summary: Animals move with a level of precision and adaptability that robots struggle to match. In Carnegie Mellon University's Department of Mechanical Engineering, researchers are developing a new AI-driven approach to uncover how brains and bodies work together
articles.selectionReason: Explores bio-inspired AI for robot control, offering insights into advanced autonomy research.

articles.title: Honeybees teach drones how to navigate
